versions.note: Beats 33% chance on volatility — but sentiment didn't help

inProgress: v2 — a stronger sentiment signal: per-headline embeddings instead of a daily average, a longer multi-year history, and testing whether sentiment helps more for direction than for volatility.

changed: First end-to-end build: a small MLP that classifies a stock's next-week risk (Low / Medium / High realised volatility) from 12 features — trailing returns, rolling volatility, volume, and a daily news-sentiment signal.
Data: Yahoo Finance prices + ticker-level news sentiment (Polygon news, 2023), 59 stocks; split by time with no look-ahead.
Exported to a 20 KB ONNX model running on-device in the browser demo.
improved: Volatility is genuinely predictable: 57% accuracy on 3 classes versus 33% for chance, driven by volatility clustering in the price features.
knownGaps: Adding sentiment did NOT help — the price-only model actually scored 0.4 points higher. That is an honest and common finding: a sparse daily-average sentiment signal is noisy, and price-based volatility features already capture most of what's predictable. v2 tries a richer sentiment signal to see if it ever earns its keep.
This predicts volatility (how much a stock moves), not direction (up or down) — and it is an educational model, never financial advice.
